entries.title: headspin-control
entries.description: HeadSpin device control for Claude Code: skills, slash commands, subagents, hooks, and a bundled MCP server (20 REST tools). Authenticate to a HeadSpin environment, enumerate and lock real devices, drive them over documented REST/Appium surfaces, and — new in 1.1 — run full capture sessions end-to-end (adb lock/shell/start/stop capture, live-validated on real devices 2026-07-03), list capture sessions, pull Waterfall issue-card reports, download session artifacts (HAR network waterfall, screen-recording MP4, device event logs), and read device time series (memory, battery, network, video-quality). Every tool is a real Bearer-REST call, live-validated against api-dev.headspin.io captured sessions on 2026-07-02 (see e2e-evidence/headspin-forge-260702/v11-report-validation/).

entries.title: larch
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow automation for Claude Code: issue-anchored `/design` (one direct-drafting flow; plan-review panel), `/bug` (consumer issue-filing helper), `/deps` issue dependency audits, and `/implement` (positional `<issue-N>`, fixed 7200s Step 2 timeout, optional `--merge`, `--forked`, `--draft`, `--no-admin-fallback`, `--no-logs-commit`, `--coder`, `--run-id`). `/implement` Preflight reads the `larch:plan` block from the issue body; `/implement` Step 5 code review always uses the unified strict panel internally (public `--panel` argv removed).

entries.title: aaron-marketing
entries.description: 120 marketing skills and 8 commands on one shared contract, arranged as a four-layer marketing operating system — Strategy (brand narrative), Channels (SEO/GEO, organic social, email, paid ads, influencer), Orchestration (product launch), and a shared Protocol layer of truth registries and memory. SEO/GEO (keyword research, content creation, technical audits, schema markup, programmatic/parasite/local SEO, site architecture, AI-traffic, monitoring), influencer marketing (audience insight, discovery, campaign planning, outreach, amplification, ROI tracking), paid ads across the ROAS loop, email marketing across the SEND loop, product launch across the RAMP loop, organic social across the ECHO loop (channel portfolio, voice dossiers, platform norm cards, calendars, platform-native creative, advocacy, social-quality audit, inbox/crisis ops, listening/SOV/dark-social measurement), and brand narrative & messaging across the TALE loop (message/positioning-truth tracing, durable message house, brand voice + naming canon, story bank, surface cascade, pitch narrative, enablement, proof packaging, the NQS gate, message testing, resonance and drift monitoring), plus a shared protocol layer (entity/creator/offer-claim/consent/launch/channel/narrative truth registries, project memory) serving all seven. Frameworks: CORE-EEAT, CITE, C3, ROAS, SEND, RAMP, ECHO, TALE.

entries.title: explainmyrepo
entries.description: Turn any GitHub repo into a beautiful, art-directed explainer site that takes a stranger from "I've never seen this" to "oh, I get why this matters — and I'm ready to use it." One Claude Code skill (the brain) reads the repo, builds a real RVF vector knowledge base, conceives a bespoke visual metaphor, authors the comprehension arc, generates raster + SVG visuals, assembles a responsive page on a shared design system, grades it on real pixels against a dual quality gate (>=95 on mobile and desktop), deploys it to its own URL, and ships a downloadable AI knowledge pack so the reader's AI gets it too. Pure, independently-testable tools do the mechanics; one BuildContext carries the work.

entries.title: frontend-skills
entries.description: React/TypeScript frontend + Go backend enforcement harness. 98 wired hooks across 108 scripts enforce patterns on every edit. Green != done: `test-warning-check` hard-blocks warnings on passing test/lint/type runs (DeprecationWarning, React `act()`, unhandled rejection, `@ts-ignore`); `ci-warning-audit` Stop-hook scans `gh run view --log` on green CI for deprecations / console warnings / skipped tests. 88 skills from TDD through CI-green PR, including /aip protobuf API design, /prime startup briefs, /thermo-nuclear-code-quality-review release-blocking PR audits, /resilience-review resilience checks, /steelman anti-sycophancy guard, /upgrade-dependency safe dependency upgrades, and /snyk-ux-security per-path vuln sweep (JS + Go ecosystems; exploitability-triage first gate via `bun why`/`go mod why`/`govulncheck`; top-level direct dep bump first, parent dep bump second, resolutions/overrides/replace as last resort only with follow-up TODO to remove; React 18 pin + changelog-walked major bumps + no-deferral escalation; Go `snyk test --file=go.mod` + `go get -u` + `go mod tidy` + `govulncheck` verify). Session exit blocked while PR review threads remain unresolved. TypeScript escape hatches blocked at Edit. tsconfig strictness weakening blocked. Worktree isolation + branch safety hook-enforced. bun.lock + yarn.lock parity enforced (Snyk IO doesn't parse bun.lock). 21 LLM failure modes enforced: 7 Karpathy single-agent + 14 MAST multi-agent (Cemri et al. NeurIPS 2025). OWASP + STRIDE subset + snyk/bun audit. Core Web Vitals perf gate. MCP ban with CLI redirect (~20x token savings). Agent-browser wrap (~91% token reduction for AI browsing). 3-hat plan review (product/engineering/design). 9 agents (adds plan hats + karpathy reference), 5 routines. Opus 4.7 tuned, POSIX-friendly.

entries.description: Nothing ships unproven. Heimdall is an autonomous multi-agent orchestrator for Claude Code that turns one prompt into a finished project — and keeps moving the bar on what counts as done: every plan wires an external, falsifiable oracle so the implementation can never grade its own homework, and the merge stays blocked until the work is proven correct. entries.description: Twingate ZTNA implementation assistant — architecture, deployment, IaC, troubleshooting. Use this plugin whenever working with Twingate, zero trust network access, or any of: connectors, remote networks, resources, security policies, identity providers, device trust, DNS filtering, exit networks, the Twingate API, Twingate Terraform provider, Twingate Pulumi provider, Twingate Kubernetes operator, Twingate gateway, or SSH PAM.

entries.title: nz-skills
entries.description: Community-contributed AI skills for New Zealand public data — LINZ, Stats NZ, Auckland Transport, GeoNet, weather, fuel prices, electricity, retail pricing, transport, and more. Drop-in read-only connectors to real NZ infrastructure and datasets.

entries.title: claudna
entries.description: Curated Claude Code skills, agents, and hooks — the canonical capability set of the Claudfather ecosystem.

entries.title: tinker
entries.description: Skills for fine-tuning language models with the Tinker API — research, debugging, and more.

entries.title: beagle
entries.description: Code review skills and verification workflows for Python, Go, React, and AI frameworks

entries.title: vibe-insights
entries.description: Vibe Insights — the deep retrospective for your Claude Code work. Coverage, where-was-I recall, token/cost (incl. the cache reveal), trends, how-you-work, ranked open threads, decisions, languages, per-session friction/satisfaction/outcome, and a synthesized narrative read. Across your full history and every machine you code on. Personal by default; optionally keep chosen sources or repos local-only. No telemetry, all local. MCP-agnostic decisions overlay.

entries.title: helium-mcp
entries.description: Remote MCP server for news bias (37 dimensions), live market data, and ML options pricing.

entries.title: whatsapp-claude-channel
entries.description: WhatsApp channel for Claude Code — linked-device messaging bridge with built-in access control. Manage pairing, allowlists, and policy via /whatsapp-claude-channel:access.

entries.title: prism
entries.description: Whole-repo code navigation (the prism-mcp server) plus defect-focused diff slicing, packaged for coding agents. Pre-wires the prism MCP server to the current project and ships two Agent Skills (prism-code-navigation, prism-code-slicing) that teach an agent when and how to use them.

entries.title: conductor
entries.description: Spec-Driven Development Orchestration Plugin for Claude Code

entries.title: onehome-mcp
entries.description: OneHome (CoreLogic) real-estate access for Claude — your agent-shared portal listings, property details, photos, schools, saved searches

entries.title: foldkit-skills
entries.description: Skills for building Foldkit apps — app generator, message scaffolding, submodel extraction, and architecture auditing

entries.title: gemini-manager
entries.description: Enables Claude Code to manage Gemini CLI as an intern. Claude acts as manager/architect while Gemini does all coding work.

entries.title: claude-skills
entries.description: Personal user-scope Claude Code skills. PKI/ops runbooks: internal-ca (2-tier PKI bootstrap with Vault, OCSP, CRL, Ansible deploy), mtls (server-trusts-client mutual TLS on top of internal-ca), lxd-claude-setup (provision Claude Code on a headless LXD/Linux server). Bare-metal Kubernetes family: k8s-bare-metal (kubeadm + Calico + Longhorn + Rancher, single-node/HA/join, checkpoint-resume), harbor-registry (private Harbor registry with self-signed CA), cicd-platform (Argo CD + self-hosted GitHub Actions runners). MJBL mTLS platform suite (the live deployed ecosystem on this host, indexing the /home/mjbl/* runbooks as source-of-truth): mjbl-mtls-platform, mjbl-ca-operations, mjbl-enrollment-plane, mjbl-client-provisioning, mjbl-cert-lifecycle, mjbl-operator-portal, mjbl-mtls-troubleshooting. Slash commands: /k8s-setup, /harbor-setup, /cicd-setup, and the MJBL suite under /mjbl:mtls:* (platform, ca, enroll, client, cert, portal, troubleshoot, issue-server). MJBL Kubernetes estate suite (the live multi-cluster topology — production rkek8s, the facility/ArgoCD cluster, UAT, and DR — kubeconfigs, MetalLB, ArgoCD external-cluster deploys, and the kubectl-apply DR tooling): mjbl-k8s-platform, mjbl-k8s-production, mjbl-k8s-facility, mjbl-k8s-dr, under /mjbl:k8s:* (platform, prod, facility, dr).

entries.title: tdder
entries.description: Guides AI agents through disciplined coding practices. Language and build-system agnostic core with extensible language/build skills.

entries.title: cybersec-toolkit
entries.description: 872 on-demand security skills for CTF, pentest, bug bounty, DFIR, detection engineering, cloud, identity, and red/blue team work. Skills are plain Markdown and activate by task without permanently consuming context. Bundles vendored skills under mixed licenses (MIT, Apache-2.0, CC-BY-SA-4.0) — see per-source attribution in .claude/skills/SKILLS.md.

entries.title: tl-telar
entries.description: Agentic engineering framework for cross-platform apps: mobile (React Native, Flutter), web (Astro, Next.js, TanStack), Rust services, and desktop - 46 agents, 116 skills, 23 commands, 4 hooks, 7 rules, 29 scripts

entries.title: scribe
entries.description: per-project・Claude Code ネイティブの実装 administrator(ユーザー代理 AI)プラグイン。substrate(cc-session/beads/doobidoo)を compose し、spawn/監視/gate/role 別文脈注入を担う orchestration 層。v0 = 堀なし軽量モード(成文化 + 道具 + 3 role SessionStart 注入)。

entries.title: tiered-development
entries.description: Multi-tier model delegation for Claude Code: the Opus coordinator brainstorms a rough plan with you, then chooses the model tier for each slice (or a Sonnet composer picks) — Opus/Fable architects refine and review, Opus builders write substantive code, Sonnet handles mechanical edits/research/verification, and Haiku the menial work. Fable is the premium tier, spent sparingly. A fresh agent per task, each with a clean context. Ships six agents, a gated coordination skill, and three helper workflows (design-panel, execute-wave, review-panel).

entries.title: tms-pipeline
entries.description: An opinionated AI-agent delivery pipeline: staged skills from ticket to review gate, with 04b loop review, multi-agent mob implementation, severity-rated gap audit, cost-tiered escort profiles, and guaranteed follow-up/launch capture.

entries.title: sema
entries.description: Content-addressed vocabulary protocol — shared meaning for AI agents

entries.title: palladius-public-goodies
entries.description: (💛) Riccardo's public Antigravity plugins and agent skills for inspiration.

entries.title: keel
entries.description: Foundation-first autonomous build workflow for Claude Code: spec the foundation, build feature-by-feature, gate every milestone with independent verification and a commit-pinned record. Stack-neutral — each project derives its own verification profile.

entries.title: dependabot-reviewer
entries.description: Review, verify, and fix Dependabot and ospo-renovate PRs across all GitHub hosts. /dependabot-review approves safe updates and sets automerge. /dependabot-verify reports PR status read-only. /dependabot-fix fixes a single selected PR or broken main-branch CI interactively — analyses autonomously, proposes a repair plan, and executes after user confirmation.

entries.title: firefox-devtools-mcp
entries.description: Control Firefox for browsing, web testing, and debugging. Fill forms, capture network and console activity, take screenshots, run scripts, and profile performance. Supports Android devices.

entries.title: journal
entries.description: Capture development history into an immutable, dated journal - decisions, research verdicts, tombstones, lessons, external sources, session summaries, reminders, drafts, and execution plans.

entries.title: xentral-connector
entries.description: Xentral Agent OS — connector and skills.

entries.description: A growing collection of practical Agent Skills. Currently: github-profile (design, build, and deploy a GitHub profile README) and github-security-audit (audit and harden GitHub account and repositories).

entries.title: fzf-mcp
entries.description: Fuzzy-find (fzf) tools (fzf-mcp server).

entries.title: paraqualis-gxp
entries.description: Life-sciences regulatory & computer-system-validation toolkit for Claude Code — GAMP 5, 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11, the live eCFR, openFDA, and an IQ/OQ/PQ qualification engine (/qualify:build, /qualify:requirements, /qualify:review). Commands, skills, sub-agents, and a document-protection hook. Run /openfda:setup once to provision a free personal openFDA API key (one minute, raises rate limits ~1,000 → ~120,000 requests/day).

entries.title: autoharness
entries.description: Per-symbol maintenance optimizer for an agent's skill layer — learns skills from real runs and keeps them by adherence in use, not by an offline eval score.

entries.title: world-cup-picks-report
entries.description: Generate Harbor-backed FIFA World Cup scoreline reports from expert picks, model probabilities, market consensus, Elo ratings, lineup checks, and knockout penalty-risk analysis.

entries.title: agent-harness
entries.description: Portable AI agent harness — curated review/build/test agents, secret-hardening + worktree + plan-gate hooks, and supervise/wrap/verify-completion skills. Install once, use in every project. AI-agnostic core (Claude Code / Codex / Gemini).

entries.title: dev-workflow
entries.description: Standalone, à-la-carte skills from the dev-workflow pipeline: minimalism, search-first, simplification, verification, security review, and more.

entries.title: sap-table-field-remediator
entries.description: Scan custom ECC ABAP for data access on tables/fields that change in an S/4HANA brownfield conversion, classify each finding by remediation complexity (T1/T2/T3), route it with a deterministic safety guard, and emit remediation-report.json. Ships a page-cited Simplification-List knowledge base over MCP for fix derivation.

entries.title: creatorwood-sushidata-gtm
entries.description: Sushidata is a context data lake for long-term AI memory. This plugin turns Claude into a Sushidata-native agent: ingest community signals from Discord, Slack, and forums; run GTM prospecting and outbound with HeyReach, Hunter, and Apify; build competitor battlecards for PMMs; analyze campaign performance with research swarms; discover ICP signals from won/lost account data; and generate personalized outreach copy. Every finding is saved to the Sushidata context lake so future sessions have full memory of what was learned.

entries.title: grafana-dashboard-builder
entries.description: Design, generate, preview, and quality-grade Grafana dashboards as code with the Grafana Foundation SDK and Playwright. Includes a rubric-based quality and taste critic.

entries.title: code-goblin-pro
entries.description: Riley's personal collection of agent skills and resources.

entries.title: brain-memory
entries.description: Brain by AIVM — governed persistent memory for Claude Code. Auto-recall on session start, ACL-filtered recall per prompt, session wrap + sync on exit; every access recorded to a tamper-evident ledger.

entries.title: ace
entries.description: AI Connect Engine — orchestrates the ACE lifecycle from idea through app building, Connect setup, LLO management, and closeout

entries.title: keel
entries.description: Keel — an AI fractional-CTO advisor for product builders who don't have a senior technical voice in the room. Eight skills across the founder journey: first-build-scope, feature-decision, tech-evaluation, architecture-review, deep-review (repo-aware four-lens flagship), enterprise-ready, estimate-check, investor-dd-prep — plus a per-project decision ledger (.keel/) that remembers decisions, assumptions, and revisit-triggers across sessions. Grounded in the persona at cto-persona.md; frameworks load on demand via frameworks/INDEX.md; prescribes first-party tools instead of rebuilding them (ecosystem-tools.md).

entries.title: expo

entries.title: cost-oriented-agentic-workflow
entries.description: Token-economy agentic workflow for Claude Code: Opus plans, routes, and adjudicates; a Sonnet subagent does the token-heavy writing; independent Sonnet reviewers gate quality by risk; the controller stays lean. Standard and production modes.

entries.title: qiongli-political-economy
entries.description: Political economy workflow for institutions, mechanisms, distribution, and comparative political-economic analysis.

entries.description: 33-skill AI-assisted development workflow ecosystem covering the full SDLC: requirements, implement, review, test, retro, interview prep, and more. Point-Line-Net three-tier orchestration.

Components auto-discover from skills/, agents/, commands/, hooks/ — do not re-add explicit component arrays here (they drift; disk is the source of truth).

Локальный standalone-стек (docker, zero-config), workspace ~/nabu, проактивные задачи, самоулучшение (Commons).

entries.title: sonarcloud-security-triaging
entries.description: Analyze SonarCloud security issues and suggest triage decisions (SAFE/FIXED/wontfix/falsepositive) with explanations. Use when reviewing security issues or making triage decisions.

entries.title: hr-wellbeing
entries.description: Help HR business partners, wellbeing program managers, and people leaders understand, design, and run employee wellbeing, mental health, and work-life balance initiatives. Use when asked to "design a wellbeing program", "run a burnout risk assessment", "build a mental health support plan", "design a flexible work policy", "respond to a wellbeing crisis", "measure employee wellbeing", "design a return-to-work plan after burnout leave", or any employee wellbeing, mental health, and work-life balance task.

entries.title: review-buddy
entries.description: AI-assisted narrative code review for Claude Code — an agent reads your PR, writes a Prologue, and segments it into risk-rated Chapters you walk through in the browser.

entries.description: Make any repo AI-native. Analyzes your codebase and generates a complete Claude Code configuration — agents, skills, rules, and hooks tailored to your stack.

entries.title: cpa
entries.description: Audit Economico's books for correctness and US GAAP conformance — tie out the statements and surface misstatements, misclassifications, missing accruals, and control gaps as ranked findings with evidence and the correcting entry. Read-only.
